utilityName: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission Backflow Prevention Program
testingFrequency: On the local certification cycle for protected assemblies
dueBasis: San Francisco pushes users to a certified tester list through SF.gov and keeps the utility contact path inside SFPUC channels.
coveredPropertyTypes: Other SFPUC accounts with backflow assemblies
officialListLabel: Open the SF.gov certified tester list for San Francisco backflow assemblies
penalties: Missing the certified-tester workflow makes the property owner more likely to stay in a vague, non-compliant state instead of an accepted SFPUC path.
sourceExcerpt: San Francisco is commercially useful because SF.gov exposes a certified tester list with real companies instead of making users guess.
verdictSummary: San Francisco is a strong approved-tester utility because SF.gov publishes a genuine certified tester list with contact details.
whoIsAffected: SFPUC customers and protected San Francisco properties whose assemblies need a certified tester from the city's accepted list.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strongest where an irrigation or other protected assembly already exists.
The city value is not generic homeowner education but clear tester routing.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is strong because the SF.gov list behaves like a real utility-approved next step.
This is a good example of official guidance and directory content staying separate while still monetizable.

annualTesting.summary: San Francisco's strongest public signal is the certified tester list itself.
highlights: The official list includes company names and contact details.
The route is an authority-first list, not a private directory.
SFPUC contact remains the support layer behind the tester routing.
workflowSteps: Confirm the property has a backflow assembly inside the SFPUC program.
Choose a certified tester from the SF.gov list.
Coordinate the accepted report through the city utility workflow.

irrigation.summary: San Francisco is useful for irrigation only when a real protected assembly exists and the owner needs a certified tester.
highlights: The official tester list is the strongest public asset.
This page captures high-intent routing better than generic irrigation advice.
The city utility contact remains available when details are unclear.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation assembly needs certification.
Use a certified tester from the official list.
Keep the report within the SFPUC process.

fireLine.summary: The official list is already geared toward certified testers rather than generic plumbers.
highlights: The certified tester list is public and specific.
Commercial and larger protected services are a natural fit for this route.
Official routing stays separate from directory inventory.
workflowSteps: Confirm the assembly type and compliance requirement.
Use a certified tester from the official list.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but the local advantage is the public certified tester list.
